sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 46

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 46:1
items.html:
(to the question) Chotam [seal]
There are two seals [Chotamot] in the Yesod of the Nukva: one in a discernment of sinking [Shekia], and the second in a discernment of protrusion [Blita]. The first is the illumination of the Gevurot that she receives from ZA, and this seal is the secret of a boundary [Techum]. The second she receives from Ima, and this seal is Chomat — that is, a wall of guarding. She receives this from the three clothings of NHY of Ima that come with her Mochin, which are the three names Ehyeh, which are Chotam [seal] in Gematria. See Answer 30. (p. 783, Item 78; and p. 798, Item 96)
